title: MM to Inches Converter
shortDescription: Convert millimeters to inches with a precise factor and clear results for measurements, drawings, and parts.
metaDescription: Convert millimeters to inches with clear decimal results for measurements, drawings, and part specifications.
intro: Enter a millimeter value and get the equivalent in inches immediately.
overview:
Use this converter when a measurement is given in millimeters but your project, drawing, or spec sheet needs inches. The tool applies the standard conversion factor of 25.4 mm per inch and shows the result in decimal inches.

It is useful for workshop notes, machine parts, product dimensions, and comparing metric sizes to imperial references. If you need a rounded answer for a label or a more exact decimal for technical work, you can read the output directly and decide how many places to keep.
howItWorks: Type a millimeter value into the input field.
The tool divides the value by 25.4 to calculate inches.
Read the decimal result and round it to match your task if needed.

useCases.title: Workshop dimensions
useCases.description: Convert a measured metal strip, board, or bracket from millimeters into inches before marking or cutting.

useCases.title: Engineering notes
useCases.description: Check a metric dimension against an inch-based drawing or specification without manual calculation.

useCases.title: Product sizing
useCases.description: Translate package, panel, or component dimensions from millimeters into inches for catalogs or labels.

examples.title: Metric part thickness
examples.description: A thin part converts to a fractional-looking decimal in inches.

examples.title: Drawing dimension
examples.description: Useful for larger measurements where decimals matter.

faq.question: What formula does this converter use?
faq.answer: It uses inches = millimeters ÷ 25.4, which is the standard metric-to-imperial conversion.

faq.question: Why do I get a decimal instead of a fraction?
faq.answer: The tool outputs decimal inches because that is the most direct form for technical measurements and calculations. You can convert the decimal to a fraction if your project requires it.

faq.question: Can I enter decimal millimeter values?
faq.answer: Yes. Values such as 12.5 mm or 0.8 mm can be converted normally and will return a decimal inch result.

faq.question: What is the most common mistake when reading the result?
faq.answer: A common mistake is treating the output as a rounded whole number. Always keep enough decimal places for the tolerance you need before rounding.
